title: Cloudflare Worker Deployment Flow
interaction_flow: --- INIT PHASE ---
WorkerService._init() → cloudflareDeploy.setCloudflareKeys(this.config): set XAUTHKEY, ACCOUNTID, namespace → builds WORKERS_BASE_URL
WorkerService._init() → svc_event.on('fs.write.file', handler): register auto-deploy listener
--- MANUAL CREATE FLOW (driver API call) ---
Client → WorkerService.create({filePath, workerName, authorization, appId}): trigger via 'workers' driver interface
WorkerService → SubdomainEntityStore.select(StartsWith 'workers.puter.'): check subdomain count (<= 100 limit)
WorkerService → svc_auth.get_user_app_token(appId): get app-scoped token [if appId provided]
WorkerService → puterUtils.getUserInfo(authorization, api_base_url): fetch userData (uuid etc.)
WorkerService → PuterFilesystem.readPuterFile(actor, filePath): stream file, max 10MB
WorkerService → SubdomainEntityStore.upsert(entity{subdomain:'workers.puter.<calcName>', root_dir:filePath}): register worker subdomain
WorkerService → cloudflareDeploy.createWorker(userData, authorization, calcName, preamble+fileData, PREAMBLE_LENGTH)
cloudflareDeploy.createWorker → FormData.append('metadata', workerMetaData{body_part:'swCode', bindings:[puter_auth, puter_endpoint]})
cloudflareDeploy.createWorker → cloudflareDeploy.cfFetch(WORKERS_BASE_URL+'/scripts/<workerName>/', 'PUT', formData)
cloudflareDeploy.cfFetch → fetch(url, {Authorization: Bearer XAUTHKEY, method, body}): call Cloudflare API
CloudflareAPI → cloudflareDeploy.cfFetch: return response JSON {success, errors, result}
cloudflareDeploy.createWorker → WorkerService: return {success:true, url:'https://<workerName>.puter.work'} OR {success:false, errors:[parsedErrors], body}
WorkerService → Client: return cfData
--- AUTO-DEPLOY FLOW (event-driven fs.write.file) ---
PuterFilesystem → WorkerService.on('fs.write.file', data): file write event fires
WorkerService → SubdomainEntityStore.select(Eq root_dir==data.node): check if file belongs to a worker
WorkerService → result.get('subdomain'): verify subdomain starts with 'workers.puter.'
WorkerService → PuterFilesystem.readPuterFile(actor, data.node.path): read updated file content
WorkerService → svc_auth.get_user_app_token(appID) OR svc_auth.create_session_token(owner): obtain auth token
WorkerService → cloudflareDeploy.createWorker(ownerUserData, authToken, workerName, preamble+fileData, PREAMBLE_LENGTH)
WorkerService → NotificationService.notify(UsernameNotifSelector, {title:'Deployed <url>' OR 'Failed to deploy'}): send result notification
--- PREAMBLE ATTACHMENT ---
WorkerService._init(): preamble = fs.readFileSync('dist/workerPreamble.js') [built by webpack from puter-portable.js]
WorkerService: PREAMBLE_LENGTH = preamble.split('\n').length - 1 [used for V8 stack trace line-offset correction]
puter-portable.js: defines globalThis.init_puter_portable(auth, apiOrigin, type): patches EventTarget for CF, inits puter.js via C #include, calls puter.setAPIOrigin + setAuthToken
puter-portable.js: #include webpackPreamplePart.js [appended at end of preamble]
createWorker: body = preamble + userFileData [concatenated before upload to CF]
--- TEST WEBHOOK TOOL (test-webhook.js, separate flow) ---
TestWebhookTool.main() → loadConfig(): read .test-webhook-config.json {key, webhook_secret, nonce}
TestWebhookTool.main() → ask(rl, ...): prompt user for instance URL
TestWebhookTool.main(): build HMAC-SHA256 signature over 'timestamp.nonce.body' using webhook_secret
TestWebhookTool → fetch(url+'/broadcast/webhook', POST, headers{X-Broadcast-Peer-Id, X-Broadcast-Timestamp, X-Broadcast-Nonce, X-Broadcast-Signature})
TestWebhookTool → saveConfig(peerId, webhookSecret, nonce+1, instanceUrl): persist incremented nonce
key_relationships: WorkerService depends_on SubdomainEntityStore (es:subdomain) for worker→file mapping
WorkerService depends_on svc_auth for token creation
WorkerService depends_on NotificationService for deploy result feedback
WorkerService depends_on svc_event for auto-deploy trigger (fs.write.file)
cloudflareDeploy.cfFetch wraps global fetch with Bearer auth header
puter-portable.js is compiled by webpack into dist/workerPreamble.js; prepended to every worker upload
init_puter_portable bundles puter.js into worker global scope and sets auth token + API origin from CF bindings
test-webhook.js is a standalone CLI tool (not part of deploy chain); tests broadcast webhook endpoint
important_notes: WORKERS_BASE_URL = CF_BASE_URL + 'client/v4/accounts/<ACCOUNTID>/workers[/dispatch/namespaces/<namespace>]' — namespace only appended if config.namespace is set (Workers for Platforms support)
PREAMBLE_LENGTH used to subtract preamble line count from V8 stack trace so errors point to user code lines
Subdomain limit: 100 workers per user enforced before create
Worker name must match /^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+$/; reserved_words checked from global_config
App-owned workers: authorization token is app-scoped (get_user_app_token); user-owned: session token (create_session_token)
Auto-deploy skips if meta.from_outside=true (avoids double-deploy on broadcast events)
puter-portable.js uses C preprocessor #include (not standard JS) — processed at build time by webpack
init_puter_portable type='userPuter' creates isolated context via with() to avoid global pollution
